categories.name: Completeness / Coverage
categories.comment: Core WI-05 behaviors are mostly present (create overlay + human-input record, cancel no-op, race handling, ineligible rejection, immutable machine stop). Coverage is reduced by partial carry-over of orchestration/dependency constraints and role/window detail from feature context, plus one missing explicit traceability mapping to source IDs/personas.

categories.name: Fidelity / Accuracy
categories.comment: The spec preserves key intent from the ticket and F-01.1 context: append-only human input model and no mutation of machine data are represented faithfully. Accuracy is reduced by under-specification of explicit retention and dependency/orchestration framing stated in source materials.

categories.name: Absence of invention
categories.comment: Most content traces cleanly to WI-05 and feature context. The success criteria introduce several un-sourced numeric targets (e.g., 95% in 30 seconds; multiple 100% thresholds) that are not present in ticket/feature/PRD inputs, creating measurable but untraceable additions.

categories.name: Consistency & terminology
categories.comment: Terminology is largely aligned (Machine Stop, human-input record, reclassify, covered/uncovered). Score is lowered because canonical BR source is unavailable in this repo snapshot and the spec does not reference BR IDs or concern-specific rule anchors, limiting consistency verification against cross-cutting rules.

categories.name: Traceability
categories.comment: The spec uses internal FR numbering, but does not map requirements back to parent source IDs (F-01.1, WI-05 ACs) or personas by ID (P-01/P-02a), despite traceability expectations in PRD/persona governance. This weakens downstream gap-check reliability.

categories.name: Testability & clarity
categories.comment: User stories and FRs are generally testable and clear, with explicit race/cancel/eligibility behaviors and outcomes. Minor deductions for a few partially bounded constraints (dependency orchestration and role/window specificity) that may require BA confirmation before implementation.

categories.name: NFR & security/permission coverage
categories.comment: This category is capped due to missing canonical NFR/security/business-rule files in workspace context (per PRD concern-precedence model). Within available inputs, audit identity/timestamp are captured, but explicit retention requirement (>=12 months) is not carried into the spec.

gating_reason: Verdict band 4: overall score 60-84 and Major findings present.

findings.evidence_source: PRD requires ID-chain traceability ('Module -> Feature -> FR -> Work Item -> specify.md') and gap-check mapping (docs/domain/PRD.md:55, docs/domain/PRD.md:65). Personas requires FR/AC trace to persona IDs (docs/domain/PERSONAS.md:4).
findings.evidence_spec: Spec has FR-001..FR-013 but no source ID mapping or persona IDs (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:69, specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:81).
findings.comment: Without explicit mapping to F-01.1/WI-05 ACs and persona IDs, downstream completeness checks can miss drift or omissions.
findings.recommendation: Add a traceability section mapping each FR to WI-05 AC item(s), F-01.1, and persona IDs (at minimum P-01; include P-02a scope note if intended).

findings.evidence_source: Ticket defines orchestrator dependency path: 'Reclassification Service ... calling Coverage Engine (01), Reason Eligibility Filter (02), and Writable Window Policy (03)' (.scratch/downtime-reclassification/issues/05-reclassify-single-stop.md:26).
findings.evidence_spec: Spec only carries this as assumption ('policies already exist') rather than a requirement/dependency contract (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:104).
findings.comment: Dependencies are present but under-specified as assumptions, which weakens enforceable behavior when implementing the slice.
findings.recommendation: Promote dependency/orchestration expectation into explicit requirements or dependency constraints section tied to WI blockers 01/02/03.

findings.evidence_source: Feature doc requires audit traceability and retention: 'every human-input record is traceable to user and timestamp (>=12-month retention, per F-07.3)' (docs/domain/features/downtime_reclassification.md:81). Feature inventory marks F-07.3 critical with >=12-month retention (docs/domain/features/FEATURE_INVENTORY.md:128).
findings.evidence_spec: Spec retains actor identity/timestamp but omits retention duration (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:81).
findings.comment: Implementation could pass feature tests while missing mandated audit-retention behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit requirement carrying retention duration and audit immutability expectations for human-input records.

findings.evidence_source: Ticket/feature inputs do not provide numeric targets for task time or absolute success percentages for this slice (see WI acceptance list and feature narrative: .scratch/downtime-reclassification/issues/05-reclassify-single-stop.md:35, docs/domain/features/downtime_reclassification.md:42).
findings.evidence_spec: Spec adds unreferenced metrics: '95% ... in 30 seconds' and multiple '100%' targets (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:95, specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:99).
findings.comment: These numbers may be useful but are currently untraceable and could become accidental commitments.
findings.recommendation: Either cite authoritative source for each threshold or replace with source-traceable, non-fabricated success outcomes.

findings.evidence_source: Feature states operators should see standardized human-readable reasons and not raw PLC codes (docs/domain/features/downtime_reclassification.md:45).
findings.evidence_spec: Spec requires eligible reasons but does not explicitly state suppression of raw PLC code display (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:70).
findings.comment: UX behavior is likely intended but not explicit, which can lead to inconsistent UI implementations.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit wording that operator-facing reason selection uses human-readable standardized reasons and excludes raw PLC reason codes.

findings.evidence_source: PRD concern precedence points to canonical cross-cutting rules and NFR/security sources for conflict resolution (docs/domain/PRD.md:44, docs/domain/PRD.md:49). These files are not present in this workspace path set.
findings.evidence_spec: Spec has no BR/NFR/SECURITY references and cannot be fully checked against missing canonical files (specs/002-reclassify-single-stop/spec.md:65).
findings.comment: Review confidence for permissions, policy constraints, and NFR/security carry-over is limited by missing canonical inputs.
findings.recommendation: Provide canonical BUSINESS_RULES.md and SECURITY/NFR artifacts (or approved equivalents) and rerun spec-check for full confidence before implementation gating.
